evidence_excerpt: He died because of sudden cardiac arrest approximately 3 months after the onset of his symptoms.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Persistent neurological symptoms included dementia, psychiatric symptoms, myoclonus, rigidity, and spasticity until death.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: His disturbed consciousness gradually improved, and MRI cortical hyper-intensity diminished within 4 weeks.
We observed progressive atrophy of the brain cortices, and he continued to have dementia, myoclonus, rigidity, and spasticity despite treatment.
He died because of sudden cardiac arrest approximately 3 months after onset.

notes_on_uncertainty: The exact index time (definitive diagnosis before treatment) was not clearly documented; admission related to the condition was used as proxy. Death occurred at ~3 months, limiting long-term (>12 months) assessment.
